summary: Branch achieves its stated security goal — System.IO is gone from action handlers, PLNG002 at error severity enforces it, F1 high-severity path-traversal is closed by ctor canonicalization. But the PLang --test suite regresses 206→204 on a clean rebuild; tester v2's 206/206 figure does not reproduce. The two failures are both in Builder/CompileLlmNotes/, both with 'Channel input has no interactive answerer (stream EOF)' — the signature of an AuthGate prompt firing against the test runner's non-interactive channel. Bisect points at 064724fda (the F1 canonicalization fix) as the introducing commit. F1 is the right shape; the test setup needs to align with it.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: agree — 5 LOWs accurate; N1/N5 closed in coder 012b1d74c; N3 footgun documented and currently uncalled.
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: disagree on PLang suite count — 206/206 claim does not reproduce on clean rebuild; actual is 204/206.
previous_reviews_assessed.security: agree — F1 mutation-tested clean, F2/F3 closed, PLNG002 carve-outs tight.

findings.issue: Full-suite PLang --test run on a clean rebuild is 204/206. Two failures: Builder/CompileLlmNotes/output-write-no-channel.test.goal and Builder/CompileLlmNotes/assert-equals-no-message.test.goal, both 'Channel input has no interactive answerer (stream EOF)'. Bisect: commit 064724fda (F1 canonicalization) introduces the regression. Runtime2 baseline is 206/206; pre-F1 commit on this branch is 206/206; F1 commit and HEAD are both 204/206. Tester v2 round-2 report claims 206/206 — wrong. The failing first step is `copy file '../../Simple/.build/start.pr' to 'start-out.txt'`; after F1 canonicalizes paths at the FilePath ctor, the resolved absolute apparently falls outside the prompt-free zone, escalating the copy to a permission prompt. F1 itself is correct — it closes a HIGH-severity path-traversal vuln — but either the test's relative path or the runtime-dir anchor in file.@this.Resolve needs to align with canonical semantics. Order-dependence (tests pass in isolation, fail in full-suite order) is a secondary symptom worth noting but not the headline.
findings.impact: The PLang suite green-bar was the explicit merge gate in coder's handoff and tester's v2 report. Shipping with that gate red and the count silently wrong lowers the bar for the next reviewer pair to reproduce the claimed baseline.
findings.suggestion: Reproduce on clean rebuild. Inspect file.@this.Resolve runtime-dir anchoring (PLang/app/types/path/file/this.cs:88-106) against Goal.GetRuntimeDirectory() for these tests. If runtime dir is the goal's .build/ subdir, the two `..` segments don't reach Tests/Simple/ post-canonicalization — fix the test's relative path (`../../../Simple/...`) and rebuild the .pr, OR adjust the runtime-dir anchor.
